Crorg A. Monning, Adminiskotive Assistont
Melbo Hoffmon, Secrelory
August 28, 1990
Mr. Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804
Dear Mr. Hallabrin:
As a member of the Riverside County Board of Supervisors,
I have been offered an opportunity to have a letter from
me included in a welcoming packet to be sent to new
residents in the Hemet area of ny District. 
This project
has been developed by private enterprise and, while I would
not be charged, participating merchants pay for having
information included in packet.
The purpose of ny }etter would be to make nehrcomers aware
of county services, facilities 
and opportunities to become
involved in their new conmunity. The letter will be sent,
at the expense of the packetrs developers, to about L2o0
new residents each guarter.
I would like to participate and pay the cost of printing
my letter from campaign funds. However, I do not intend
it as a campaign activity and I am requesting a written
FPPC opinion of whether or not it would be considered a
campaign mailing.
Thank you for your consideration.
KC: j Im: fg
4O8O Lemon Street, Fourteenth Floor. Riverside Colifornio 9250.l . 714-787-2630

sections.question: Would including a letter from me in a welcoming packet to be sent to new residents in the Hemet area, paid for from campaign funds, be considered a campaign mailing?
sections.question_synthetic: A Riverside County Supervisor seeks FPPC guidance on whether a letter to new residents in her district, to be included in a privately-developed welcoming packet and paid for with campaign funds, would constitute a campaign mailing under FPPC regulations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a government official to the FPPC, not an FPPC advice letter response. No QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections are formally marked. The question is extracted from the body of the letter. No FPPC response is included in this document.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Would including a letter from me in a welcoming packet to be sent to new residents in the Hemet area, paid for from campaign funds, be considered a campaign mailing?

This project has been developed by private enterprise and, while I would not be charged, participating merchants pay for having information included in packet. The purpose of ny }etter would be to make nehrcomers aware of county services, facilities and opportunities to become involved in their new conmunity. The letter will be sent, at the expense of the packetrs developers, to about L2o0 new residents each guarter. I would like to participate and pay the cost of printing my letter from campaign funds.
